meta.title: Nagila Decastro
meta.description: Mother of two girls, wife, homeschool educator and mentor to mothers. Helping families rest better and live motherhood with presence.

quote.text: The home cared for first holds everything else up.

body: I am Nagila — mother to Helena and Catharina, wife to Ewerton, living on Cape Cod. Every birth rewrote what I understood about rest, presence and home.
I learned firsthand that a baby's sleep is not a technical detail — it is the oxygen of an entire family. When a mother sleeps again, she returns to herself.
I chose to educate my daughters at home not as a rejection of the world, but as a deep yes to time — the kind of time that does not come twice.
Today I walk alongside mothers who want more than to survive motherhood. Mothers who want to live inside it, with clarity, ease and purpose.
Helena arrived in 2021. Catharina in 2024. Ewerton in 2017.
Before any mother I walk alongside, there is this home. It is the home I speak from, the one I return to, the place where everything I teach was first lived.

services.sectionSubtitle: Three fronts, one root: caring for the mother is caring for the whole house.

cards.description: A human, personalized plan for your baby's sleep — honoring the bond, the child's temperament and your family's real rhythm. No harsh formulas, no guilt.
Mentoring for mothers in transition: first child, second child, identity after birth, marriage after baby. Honest talk, practical tools, gentle support.
For mothers who feel the call to homeschool but don't know where to start. Routine, materials, a simple curriculum and the courage for the first week.

instagram.subtitle: I share daily what I'm learning as a mother, wife, home educator and whole woman. No filter, no pre-packaged recipe.
